title: balance() requires caller to fetch scan_tip separately — two async round-trips for one logical query
description: What is awkward: every caller must do let tip = store.scan_tip().await?; let b = store.balance(tip, N).await?; Two pool round-trips for a single logical query. The tip parameter is always scan_tip() in production; nobody passes a synthetic tip except tests. Suggested rewrite: add balance_confirmed(min_confirmations: u64) that fetches scan_tip internally in a single query (WITH tip AS (SELECT tip_height FROM scan_state WHERE id=1)). Keep balance(scan_tip, min_confirmations) for tests. This also eliminates the time-of-check race where scan_tip advances between the two calls, though the single-connection pool makes the race benign today.
close_reason: Added balance_with_confirmations(min_confirmations) convenience wrapper; keeps balance(scan_tip, min_confirmations) for tests
